hypothesis: E615's byte-identical control-vs-treatment result (schema_role_slot_decode_weight applied to object-frame properties) was attributed to an 8-step checkpoint too undertrained to emit any parseable program. Training a fresh scratch checkpoint for 10x more steps (80 vs 8) on the same corpus should be enough to produce non-empty, syntactically valid OOD predictions, letting the matched control/treatment eval pair actually exercise (or definitively fail to exercise) the E615 object-frame lever.
environment_note: This is a fresh container with an empty outputs/ dir (same starting condition as E615's session): no prior E608-E614 lineage checkpoint (e569-e561-matched-cont48-r1-48s) is recoverable. torch was not preinstalled in this sandbox and was installed via `uv sync --extra dev --extra torch` (~75s, one-time) before any training command. Node deps for AgentV publishing (`npm ci` at repo root and in `src/apps/openui_bridge`, both run with NODE_OPTIONS unset because the ambient `--import tsx` value breaks plain `npm ci`) were also installed as prerequisites, matching E615's own documented prerequisite fixes.

checkpoint.sync_reason: Explicit local-only 80-step scratch diagnostic checkpoint (E540-E548/E615 fallback style); --no-sync-checkpoints.

training.comparison_to_e615_checkpoint: E615's 8-step scratch checkpoint reached loss 37.9045 and emitted 0/4 non-empty predictions under strict grammar decode. This 80-step checkpoint reaches loss 26.5243 (10x the steps, same corpus/seed/architecture) and emits 4/4 syntactically valid, non-empty predictions.

eval_recipe.matched_to: E614/E615 recipe weights, replayed control (schema_role_slot_decode_weight=0) vs treatment (=8.0) on the same fresh 80-step checkpoint
eval_recipe.context_backend_deviation_from_e614: Same documented deviation as E615: this session's checkpoint is trained with context_backend=scratch, so eval uses context_backend=scratch (checkpoint context-tower architecture is backend-specific).

prediction_parity.note: All 4 OOD predictions are byte-identical between control and treatment, but unlike E615 they are non-empty and syntactically valid. Record ood_gallery_01 (the one exercising the E615 lever) decodes to `v0 = ImageGallery([])` in both arms: the typed array closes empty before any item object is ever opened. Because the object-frame `schema_role_slot_decode_weight` branch added in E615 (`src/slm_training/models/twotower.py::_schema_role_slot_bias`, `frame.kind == "object"`) only fires once a `{` has been opened inside a typed-array item, the lever's precondition is never reached on this checkpoint, so control and treatment are mechanically forced to agree regardless of the weight.

gallery_bug_check.target: Whether ImageGallery item src/alt/details each bind to their own matching visible slot instead of collapsing to one (E614's narrative bug), now observable end-to-end since E616 has an under-array checkpoint that parses.
gallery_bug_check.reason: The array itself never opens an item object (`ImageGallery([])`), which is a strictly earlier decode decision than the one E615's lever biases. This reproduces E612's already-documented and already-rejected finding (an undertrained/near-random checkpoint prefers closing a typed array empty over opening an item) rather than a new defect. The E615 fix is verified only at the unit level (tests/test_models/test_compiler_decode.py, unchanged and still passing this session).

comparison_to_e615.note: E615's 8-step checkpoint produced 0/4 non-empty predictions in both arms (byte-identical because nothing decodes). This 80-step checkpoint produces 4/4 syntactically valid, non-empty predictions in both arms (still byte-identical, but for a diagnosable structural reason: the Gallery array stays empty). More training steps moved the failure mode from 'no parse at all' to 'parses, but never opens a typed-object item', which is progress in diagnosability even though neither checkpoint can demonstrate the E615 fix end-to-end.

decision: Reject for promotion/ship. The control-vs-treatment eval remains quality-neutral and byte-identical on this checkpoint, now because the ImageGallery array closes empty rather than because decode fails outright. Retain the E615 code change (already unit-verified, no regression) as the current scratch policy. Retain this run as documented evidence that reaching 'array opens a typed-object item' requires either (a) a much more heavily trained/warm-started checkpoint (E608-E614's lineage, unrecoverable here) or (b) revisiting the typed-array-nonempty decision itself, which E612 already investigated and rejected as a separate lever.
next: Two independent follow-ups are now well-motivated: (1) when a sufficiently trained successor checkpoint exists (HF-context, warm-started), replay this exact matched control/treatment pair to observe the src/alt/details fix once the array actually opens an item; (2) revisit why `semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight=2.0` does not prevent an empty-array close on this checkpoint class specifically for authored (non-repeated) typed-array items like ImageGallery.images, distinguishing it from E611's repeated-plan-family slot allocation which does hold on similarly-scaled checkpoints.

failures: diagnostic OOD n=4 is below the required full-suite sample size
meaningful_program_rate and binding_aware_meaningful_v2_rate_strict are 0.0 for both arms
smoke, held_out, adversarial, and rico_held suites were not run

verification.verify_version_stamps_check: version-stamps: ok (vs HEAD; 0 changed file(s), 0 component(s) touched) -- no source changed this iteration, eval/training only
verification.prerequisite_fixes: uv sync --extra dev --extra torch (torch not preinstalled); npm ci (repo root) and npm ci (src/apps/openui_bridge), both with NODE_OPTIONS unset to work around an ambient --import tsx value that breaks npm ci in this sandbox.
